title: When Using Numerical Data: - Ask Yourself:
subtitle: Question Data in New Contexts
description:
When using numerical data:
- Ask yourself: "Does this data apply to this specific situation?"
- Double-check assumptions: Ensure the numbers make sense in the new context.
- Explore alternatives: Seek additional information before drawing conclusions.

Example: Using an old formula for a new project? Verify if it fits the current conditions first.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify the key numerical claims relevant to your current project. Gather data sources and establish a baseline for comparison.

howTo.title: Run the five-question context check
howTo.summary: Evaluate each numerical claim by asking about its source, population, process conditions, covariates, and sensitivity. This helps ensure the data is applicable.

howTo.title: Conduct a sensitivity analysis
howTo.summary: Test how changes in the numerical data affect your conclusions. Adjust the values by ±20% to see if the decision shifts significantly.

howTo.title: Schedule a micro-experiment
howTo.summary: If the sensitivity analysis indicates potential issues, plan a small pilot or test to gather real data before making larger decisions.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and insights in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/data-context-fit-checker/.
